level_signals: 5+ years in strategy & operations, consulting, CS operations, or revenue operations
2+ years managing high-performing CS, operations, or activation teams
Manages a team of 6–8 Scaled CSMs
Owns end-to-end partner activation strategy and cross-functional alignment

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Strong AI fluency. You actively use AI tools (LLMs, copilots, automation platforms) in your daily work and have a point of view on where AI should — and shouldn't — replace human touchpoints in a customer journey.

5+ years in strategy & operations, consulting, CS operations, or revenue operations within high-growth B2B SaaS — with a track record of designing operating models, defining metrics, and building scalable programs that measurably improve activation, retention, or expansion.
2+ years managing high-performing CS, operations, or activation teams.
3+ years working in or closely with channel partnerships — whether in partner program design, partner GTM motions, co-selling, or partner enablement.

Strong financial acumen. You understand how Controllers, CFOs, and finance teams evaluate and adopt software. You're conversant in ERP ecosystems, accounting workflows, and the language of financial operations — and you can translate that understanding into activation strategies that resonate with finance buyers.
Sharp analytical instincts. You build dashboards, synthesize data into insight, and make decisions with numbers — not gut feel.
Demonstrated experience designing or managing customer activation or onboarding programs at scale.
Proven cross-functional leadership — the ability to influence senior stakeholders across Product, Partnerships, Growth, Sales, Data, and CS without direct authority.
Comfort with ambiguity and the drive to build v1 programs from the ground up.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: CPA, CMA, or other financial certification — or equivalent depth of experience working inside finance organizations.
Direct experience selling to, onboarding, or supporting Controllers, CFOs, or finance teams in a B2B SaaS context.
Customer-facing or quota-carrying experience with a track record of exceeding targets.
Experience building scaled education content, webinars, or training programs.
Familiarity with product-led or hybrid-touch CS models.
Hands-on experience with AI/ML applications in customer success — e.g., predictive health scoring, AI-generated outreach, automated segmentation.

scope.surface: Partner activation and early retention for partner-referred customers; end-to-end partner activation funnel, CS segmentation, and scaled onboarding programs
core_responsibilities: Own activation and early-retention strategy for partner-referred customers
Design CS segmentation strategies using data modeling and AI-driven scoring
Align partner activation roadmap with company-level goals cross-functionally
Identify whitespace opportunities to increase multi-product attach rates
Build, iterate, and optimize scalable CS programs across thousands of customers
Leverage AI tooling, workflow automation, and predictive analytics to diagnose activation bottlenecks
Partner with Product to improve self-onboarding features and activation tooling
Manage, coach, and develop a team of 6–8 Scaled CSMs
Drive team performance against activation rate, early retention, and CSAT targets
Build AI fluency across the team
Hire, train, and enable new team members

green_flags: Clearly defined team size (6–8 CSMs) and concrete 90-day and 6-month success milestones
Explicit 'build role' framing signals real ownership and autonomy
Ramp is a high-growth fintech with $100B+ annualized spend flowing through platform — meaningful scope
Unlimited AI token usage as a benefit signals company commitment to AI tooling
Broad benefits including 100% medical coverage, 401k match, parental leave, fertility support
Relocation support to NYC or SF offered
red_flags: Visa sponsorship and work authorization policy not stated
Hybrid/office expectation implied by relocation support to NYC or SF but office time requirement not specified
Role spans strategy, operations, people management, AND technical AI fluency — broad scope may indicate under-resourcing

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from team size, years required, and cross-functional scope)
location.type (inferred as hybrid from relocation support to NYC/SF; not explicitly stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and partner channel context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities and success criteria; not enumerated as a list in JD)
compensation.equity_mentioned (no equity mentioned anywhere in JD; defaulted to false)
ambiguities: Location is not explicitly defined as remote, hybrid, or onsite in the JD body. Relocation support to NYC or SF is mentioned in benefits, suggesting in-office presence is expected, but no explicit office time requirement is stated. Treated as hybrid with cities NYC and SF.
Work authorization and visa sponsorship are not addressed anywhere in the JD.
